correlation_reasons: Alerted nodes are directly adjacent (1-2 hops)

causal_evidence.summary: 4 alert(s) within a 11-minute window (t=0..11 min), consistent with a propagating fault.
Alert type sequence matches propagation chain: cpu -> latency. This ordering is consistent with fault propagation from the initiating node.
Alerted nodes (APP-LB-01, APP-01, APP-02, DB-MASTER) are topologically proximate (topology score 0.95), suggesting fault propagation through connected infrastructure.
Hypothesis: fault initiated at APP-LB-01 (alert: cpu_spike) and propagated to 1 downstream node(s) matching known propagation patterns.

metadata.title: Known Resolution: Application Load Balancer Backend Health Degradation

retrieved_kb_evidence.text:
[Known Resolution: Application Load Balancer Backend Health Degradation] Root Cause Confirmed
The confirmed root cause in prior occurrences was one of the following:

1. **Asymmetric connection drain**: A backend member was removed from the pool without a connection drain period, causing in-flight requests to fail and the load balancer to log them as `connection_timeout`.
2. **Health check misconfiguration after a deployment**: A new application version changed the health check endpoint path but the load balancer health check configuration was not updated, causing all members to fail health checks immediately after deployment.
3. **Database-tier cascade**: DB-MASTER latency caused application backend members to exceed their own timeout thresholds, appearing to the load balancer as unhealthy members.

metadata.title: Known Resolution: Application Load Balancer Backend Health Degradation

retrieved_kb_evidence.text:
[Known Resolution: Application Load Balancer Backend Health Degradation] Incident Summary
A recurring pattern has been observed where the APP-LB-01 load balancer reports `backend_pool_unhealthy` and `cpu_spike` alerts concurrently, with downstream `connection_timeout` alerts appearing on application server nodes within 3–5 minutes.

metadata.title: Database Master Node Latency and Connection Timeout Response

retrieved_kb_evidence.text:
[Database Master Node Latency and Connection Timeout Response] Trigger Symptoms
- `cpu_spike` alert on DB-MASTER concurrent with `latency` or `connection_timeout` alerts on application nodes.
- Database connection pool utilisation exceeds 90% on application servers.
- `lock_wait_timeout` or `replication_lag` alerts on the database tier.
- Application-tier health checks begin failing with "connection refused" or "too many connections" errors.
- Active database session count is at or above the configured `max_connections` limit.
